contract.title: Marijuana rescheduled by December 31, 2026?
contract.resolution_criteria:
This market will resolve to 'Yes' if marijuana is rescheduled down from Schedule I
to any other level (or is unscheduled completely) by the Drug Enforcement Agency of
the United States by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will
resolve to 'No'.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the U.S. government,
however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

Note: In April 2026, FDA-approved marijuana products and state-licensed medical
cannabis were moved to Schedule III under President Trump's executive order. This
market specifically tracks whether recreational marijuana (currently Schedule I) is
rescheduled. If the DEA reschedules only specific cannabinoids or derivatives without
reclassifying marijuana as a whole, that does not qualify.

A DEA administrative hearing on full rescheduling is scheduled to begin June 29, 2026.

settlement_entities.name: Department of Health and Human Services
White House Office of National Drug Control Policy

conditions.label: DEA lowers 'marihuana' to II–V
conditions.summary: By Dec 31, 2026, DEA finalizes a rule/order moving the CSA entry covering recreational marijuana/marihuana (the plant as a whole) from Schedule I to II–V. Actions limited to specific cannabinoids don’t count.

conditions.label: DEA unschedules 'marihuana'
conditions.summary: By Dec 31, 2026, DEA finalizes a rule/order removing the CSA entry covering recreational marijuana/marihuana (the plant as a whole) from the schedules (decontrolled/unscheduled).

contract.narrative: The timeline shows only unrelated state or territorial notices (CA social-media rules, PR insurance forms, CA voting guidance) and no federal scheduling steps. Rescheduling pressure rests with DEA/HHS rulemaking; a final DEA rule, OMB review, and potential litigation are still pending before Dec 31, 2026.

timeline.title: California Department of Justice Releases Proposed "Protecting Our Kids from Social Media Addiction Act (SB 976)” Regulations - 14.05.2026
timeline.regulator: California Department of Justice — Consumer Protection Section
timeline.one_line_why: California social media regulations are unrelated to the federal DEA process and do not affect whether marijuana is rescheduled.

timeline.title: California Department of Justice Releases Proposed "Protecting Our Kids from Social Media Addiction Act (SB 976)” Regulations
timeline.regulator: California Department of Justice — Consumer Protection Section
timeline.one_line_why: California social-media regulation proposal is unrelated to federal DEA marijuana scheduling and provides no information on DEA action.

timeline.title: CN-2026-377-AS (Enmendada) - 12.05.2026 - Formulario y Tarifas que Serán Efectivo a Partir del Año Calendario 2027
timeline.regulator: Oficina del Comisionado de Seguros de Puerto Rico
timeline.one_line_why: Puerto Rico insurance forms/fees rule is unrelated to DEA federal scheduling of marijuana and does not affect rescheduling odds.

timeline.title: Attorney General Bonta and Secretary of State Weber: Vote Early in June Primary Election - May 5, 2026
timeline.one_line_why: California voting guidance for a June primary is unrelated to federal DEA rulemaking on marijuana rescheduling.

timeline.title: Attorney General Bonta and Secretary of State Weber in Sacramento: Vote Early in June Primary Election and Know Your Voting Rights
timeline.one_line_why: California officials urging voters in a state primary is unrelated to the federal DEA rescheduling process and doesn't change rescheduling odds.

primary_drivers: Surge in newly detected critical issues
Multiple high-severity incidents opened this week
Sharp week-over-week increase in unresolved critical risk
heat_panel.explainer: Critical heat (83.92) is driven by a sudden influx of unresolved high-severity findings and multiple new incidents this week, causing a large week-over-week spike in risk exposure.
